cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to find how many positive integers n ≤ 500 satisfy the condition that when the sum of the first n cubes is divided by n + 1, the remainder is n. Let me try to break this down step by step. First, let me recall the formula for the sum of the first n cubes. I remember that the sum from k=1 to n of k³ is equal to the square of the sum from k=1 to n of k. The formula for the sum of the first n integers is n(n + 1)/2, so squaring that gives the sum of the cubes. Therefore: Sum = [n(n + 1)/2]² So, the problem says that when this sum is divided by n + 1, the remainder is n. In mathematical terms, this can be written using modular arithmetic: [n(n + 1)/2]² ≡ n mod (n + 1) Let me write that down: ([n(n + 1)/2]²) ≡ n (mod n + 1) Hmm, okay. Let me try to simplify the left-hand side modulo n + 1. Since we're working modulo n + 1, any multiple of n + 1 will be congruent to 0.
